title: Everyone Should Know SIMD
excerpt: SIMD has a reputation for being complex. I've met many very good software engineers who dismiss it as something too complex to learn or a niche optimization meant for only the highest-performance software, not useful in everyday programming.
content_html:
SIMD has a reputation for being complex. I've met many very good software engineers who dismiss it as something too complex to learn or a niche optimization meant for only the highest-performance software, not useful in everyday programming.

I think that's wrong. SIMD can be simple to understand1, and common "process N values at a time" SIMD code to speed up a naive for loop almost always follows the same general shape. Once you learn the basics, writing SIMD is just about as easy as a for loop. And when it's not, it's usually a good sign to skip it for now.

Every developer should know at least that much SIMD.

This post uses Zig for examples but is a general piece that applies to any programming language. Support for SIMD instructions varies by programming language and I hope that more programming languages expose these generic concepts in the future!

I hate that I have to do this for every post now, but I also want to note this was completely hand-written with no AI assistance.

Table of Contents
  • Background: What Is SIMD?
  • The Common Shape
  • A Real Example
  • Step 1: Broadcast Constants
  • Step 2: Loop One Vector at a Time
  • Step 3: Perform the SIMD Operation
  • Step 4: Reduce the Vector Result
  • Step 5: Finish with the Scalar Tail
  • Recap: The Common Shape
  • Why Can't the Compiler Do This?
  • Everyone Should Know SIMD

Background: What Is SIMD?

If you already know what SIMD is, skip this section.

SIMD allows a CPU to operate on multiple values in parallel. For example, instead of comparing one byte at a time, a CPU can compare 4, 8, or even more bytes with a single instruction.

If you ever see loops like this in your code:

for (byte in bytes) { /* ... */ }
for (character in string) { /* ... */ }
for (value in array) { /* ... */ }

There is an opportunity to use SIMD. SIMD turns those into this:

for (8 byte chunk in bytes) { /* ... */ }

This results in a localized speedup that directly maps to the parallelism: you process data 4x, 8x, or even faster.

The only real requirement for this to pay off is that you need to be regularly processing a large enough number of bytes. If you're doing these for loops across data that is only ever a handful or dozens of bytes, it's not worth it. But if this is iterating over hundreds, thousands, millions of bytes, the payoff will be huge.

That's the basics. Projects such as simdutf and simdjson take this to an extreme and use SIMD techniques that can be difficult to understand. But you do not need to write algorithms like those to benefit from SIMD. The common case is dramatically simpler.

The Common Shape

The common "process N values at a time" SIMD code follows the same five steps:

  1. Broadcast any constants you need and initialize vector accumulators, if any.
  2. Loop over input one vector-width chunk at a time.
  3. Perform the comparison or arithmetic across all lanes in parallel.
  4. Reduce or store the vector result as needed.
  5. Handle the remaining elements with a scalar tail. A scalar tail is just your normal loop from before vectorizing, but it only processes the remainder that doesn't fit into a full vector.

As you do this more and more, you'll begin to naturally decompose every for loop into these five steps and writing SIMD becomes nearly as natural as writing a scalar loop.

A Real Example

Let's look at a real example from Ghostty. We'll look at the scalar implementation, the SIMD implementation, and then map it back to the common shape above.

I have a slice of decoded codepoints that I want to consume until I see a value at or below 0xF (a C0 control character).2 Terminals are mostly plain characters to be printed, so we try to batch all those together. So this loop finds the end of the next printable run as quickly as possible.

The scalar loop is one line:

while (end < cps.len and cps[end] > 0xF) end += 1;

It processes one codepoint at a time. It is easy to understand.

Here is the generic vector version with no CPU-specific intrinsics3 and no comments. I will explain it in detail later.

if (simd.lanes(u32)) |lanes| {
    const V = @Vector(lanes, u32);
    const threshold: V = @splat(0xF);
    while (end + lanes <= cps.len) : (end += lanes) {
        const values: V = cps[end..][0..lanes].*;
        const greater_than_threshold = values > threshold;
        if (@reduce(.And, greater_than_threshold)) continue;
        const mask: std.meta.Int(.unsigned, lanes) = @bitCast(greater_than_threshold);
        end += @ctz(~mask);
        break;
    }
}

while (end < cps.len and cps[end] > 0xF) end += 1;

12 more lines of code.

This can improve the loop's throughput by up to 4x with ARM NEON (including Apple Silicon), 8x with AVX2 (most modern x86 CPUs), and 16x with AVX-512 (some Intel CPUs and AMD Zen 4 and newer).

In real-world end-to-end throughput from terminal program to finalized terminal state on an AVX2 Intel desktop, this was more like a 5x speedup. You always lose some of the ideal speedup due to the other stuff around the SIMD code, but... that's still 5x!

Okay, now I understand that those 12 lines are going to look really alien to someone not familiar with the concepts. So now let's back up and explain it step by step, mapping it directly to the shape previously mentioned.

Step 1: Broadcast Constants

Let's start with the first three lines:

if (simd.lanes(u32)) |lanes| {
    const V = @Vector(lanes, u32);
    const threshold: V = @splat(0xF);

simd.lanes(u32) is a helper in Ghostty that returns the number of u32 values the target CPU can process at once. These individual values are called lanes. On ARM this returns 4, AVX2 returns 8, and AVX-512 returns 16. If the target doesn't have a vector size we want to use, it returns null and we skip all of this code and do zero SIMD work.

@Vector(lanes, u32) creates the vector type. If lanes is 8, then V is a single value containing eight u32 values that the CPU can operate on in parallel. And so on.

Finally, we need to compare every value to 0xF. A vector comparison requires a vector on both sides, so @splat(0xF) copies, or broadcasts, 0xF into every lane. The result is a vector that looks like this:

{ 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF }

This is step 1: prepare the vector type and broadcast any constants. Some algorithms also initialize a vector accumulator here, but this algorithm doesn't need one.

Step 2: Loop One Vector at a Time

Next, we loop over one complete vector at a time:

while (end + lanes <= cps.len) : (end += lanes) {
    const values: V = cps[end..][0..lanes].*;

If lanes is 8, we only enter the loop when at least eight values remain. Inside the loop, we load those eight values into the vector values. At the end of every loop, end += lanes moves forward by eight values instead of one.

The requirement for a complete vector is important. If only five values remain, we can't load an eight-lane vector. There are various tricks to handle this, but we do the easy thing and handle them via our scalar tail, which I'll explain later in step 5.

This is step 2: load and loop over the input one vector-width chunk at a time. You can see the lane-count speedup here!

Step 3: Perform the SIMD Operation

Now we perform the comparison:

const greater_than_threshold = values > threshold;

Both values and threshold are vectors, so this maps to a vector operation (a literal vector CPU instruction). The one > compares every lane in values to every corresponding lane in threshold. If there are eight lanes, this is equivalent to performing the scalar comparison cps[end] > 0xF eight times, but it does it in one CPU instruction instead.4

The result is another vector with one boolean per lane. Conceptually, it looks something like this:

values:                 { 0x41, 0x42, 0x43, 0x0A, 0x44, 0x45, 0x46, 0x47 }
threshold:              {  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF }
greater_than_threshold: { true, true, true, false, true, true, true, true }

This is the actual SIMD operation. There is no explicit inner loop. The > operator applies to every lane in parallel.

Comparisons are only one example. This could be addition, multiplication, minimum, maximum, or any other operation supported by the vector type. The point is the code still has the same shape.

Step 4: Reduce the Vector Result

We now have a vector of booleans, but the original loop needs to know the location of the first value at or below 0xF.

First, let's handle the common case where every value is above 0xF:

if (@reduce(.And, greater_than_threshold)) continue;

@reduce(.And, ...) combines every boolean using and and returns a single boolean. If every lane is true, we continue and process the next vector. In our example, lane 3 is false, so @reduce returns false and we fall through to find exactly which lane failed.

If any lane is false, then we need to find exactly which lane failed:

const mask: std.meta.Int(.unsigned, lanes) = @bitCast(greater_than_threshold);
end += @ctz(~mask);
break;

@bitCast turns the vector of booleans into an integer with one bit per lane. A 1 bit means the value was greater than 0xF and a 0 means it wasn't. We invert the mask so failed comparisons are 1, and then @ctz counts the number of zero bits before the first failure. That count is the index of the first failing lane.

We add that index to end and break because we found the control character.

Using the same values from step 3, we can see this transformation per lane:

values:                 { 0x41, 0x42, 0x43, 0x0A, 0x44, 0x45, 0x46, 0x47 }
greater_than_threshold: { true, true, true, false, true, true, true, true }
mask:                   {    1,    1,    1,     0,    1,    1,    1,    1 }
~mask:                  {    0,    0,    0,     1,    0,    0,    0,    0 }

@ctz(~mask) counts three zero bits before the first 1, so it returns 3. Adding 3 to end points it at lane 3, which contains 0x0A, the first control character.

This is step 4: reduce the vector result into whatever the original algorithm needs. This is also the step that varies the most between algorithms. A sum might reduce a vector accumulator into a single number. A transform might store the entire vector to an output buffer. Our scan turns the vector into a bit mask so it can find one specific lane.

Step 5: Finish with the Scalar Tail

After the vector loop, we run the exact scalar loop we started with:

while (end < cps.len and cps[end] > 0xF) end += 1;

If the input length isn't an exact multiple of the vector width, this processes the remaining values. For example, an eight-lane vector loop leaves anywhere from zero to seven values for this loop. This is called the scalar tail.

This loop also handles CPUs where simd.lanes(u32) returns null. In that case we skip all of the SIMD code and the scalar loop processes the entire input. The original implementation remains both the fallback and the tail.

That's step 5. It's just the normal loop.

Recap: The Common Shape

Let's map the entire implementation back to the five steps:

  1. @splat(0xF) broadcasts the comparison value into every lane.
  2. The while loop loads lanes values at a time.
  3. values > threshold compares every lane in parallel.
  4. @reduce, @bitCast, and @ctz find the first failed comparison.
  5. The original scalar loop handles the remainder and unsupported CPUs.

The details in step 4 initially take some time to understand, but the overall shape is straightforward. And steps 1, 2, 3, and 5 tend to look nearly identical across completely different algorithms.

Whenever you see a for (byte in bytes), this is the shape you'll map to.

Why Can't the Compiler Do This?

Sometimes it can! Compilers can auto-vectorize simple loops, particularly regular arithmetic loops without complex control flow. You should always compile the scalar version with optimizations and see what your compiler produces before manually writing SIMD.

But compilers are severely limited in what they can auto-vectorize and are in general very poor at it. Auto-vectorization has been an active area of compiler research for decades, and recent research still begins from the observation that production compilers regularly miss vectorization opportunities. This isn't a problem I expect to disappear soon.

More importantly, when this loop matters enough for me to care about a 5x speedup, I want the vectorization to be explicit and predictable. I don't want an unrelated code change or compiler update to quietly turn it back into a scalar loop.

Everyone Should Know SIMD

Every developer should be able to recognize the opportunity and, most importantly, should not be scared of SIMD. If you see a hot loop scanning, comparing, counting, or transforming a large amount of contiguous data, you should be able to imagine processing it a vector-width chunk at a time.

This post demonstrates that these common cases follow a very regular pattern that you quickly get used to. And with good language support, you don't need to know any assembly or CPU-specific quirks to get easy improvements.

Everyone should know SIMD enough to do this.5

Footnotes

  1. Very impressive projects like simdutf and simdjson use extremely complex SIMD tricks to achieve their goals. But this isn't what I'd consider "everyday SIMD." ↩

  2. C0 controls extend beyond 0xF. This is the cutoff Ghostty uses for this specific code path; ESC and other control-sequence handling happens elsewhere. ↩

  3. Generic vectors remove the CPU-specific syntax, not CPU-specific code generation. Zig still lowers these operations to the instruction set enabled for the target. Ghostty falls back to scalar code when it can't choose a supported vector width. ↩

  4. The comparison itself is one vector operation. Loading the vector, reducing the result, and locating the failed lane require additional instructions. The important part is that we're doing multiple comparisons at once. ↩

  5. This post was based on a Lobsters comment I wrote. ↩

blocks.html_en: If you already know what SIMD is, skip this section.

sentences.en: If you already know what SIMD is, skip this section.
sentences.zh: 如果您已经知道 SIMD 是什么，请跳过本节。

blocks.html_en: SIMD allows a CPU to operate on multiple values in parallel. For example, instead of comparing one byte at a time, a CPU can compare 4, 8, or even more bytes with a single instruction.

sentences.en: SIMD allows a CPU to operate on multiple values in parallel.
sentences.zh: SIMD 允许 CPU 并行操作多个值。

sentences.en: For example, instead of comparing one byte at a time, a CPU can compare 4, 8, or even more bytes with a single instruction.
sentences.zh: 例如，CPU 可以使用一条指令比较 4、8 甚至更多字节，而不是一次比较一个字节。

blocks.html_en: If you ever see loops like this in your code:

sentences.en: If you ever see loops like this in your code:
sentences.zh: 如果您在代码中看到这样的循环：

blocks.html_en: There is an opportunity to use SIMD. SIMD turns those into this:

sentences.en: There is an opportunity to use SIMD.
sentences.zh: 有机会使用SIMD。

sentences.en: SIMD turns those into this:
sentences.zh: SIMD 将它们变成这样：

blocks.html_en: This results in a localized speedup that directly maps to the parallelism: you process data 4x, 8x, or even faster.

sentences.en: This results in a localized speedup that directly maps to the parallelism: you process data 4x, 8x, or even faster.
sentences.zh: 这会导致局部加速，直接映射到并行性：您处理数据的速度提高了 4 倍、8 倍，甚至更快。

blocks.html_en: The only real requirement for this to pay off is that you need to be regularly processing a large enough number of bytes. If you're doing these for loops across data that is only ever a handful or dozens of bytes, it's not worth it. But if this is iterating over hundreds, thousands, millions of bytes, the payoff will be huge.

sentences.en: The only real requirement for this to pay off is that you need to be regularly processing a large enough number of bytes.
sentences.zh: 要获得回报，唯一真正的要求是您需要定期处理足够多的字节。

sentences.en: If you're doing these for loops across data that is only ever a handful or dozens of bytes, it's not worth it.
sentences.zh: 如果您在只有少数或几十个字节的数据上执行这些 for 循环，那么这是不值得的。

sentences.en: But if this is iterating over hundreds, thousands, millions of bytes, the payoff will be huge.
sentences.zh: 但如果迭代超过数百、数千、数百万字节，回报将是巨大的。

blocks.html_en: That's the basics. Projects such as simdutf and simdjson take this to an extreme and use SIMD techniques that can be difficult to understand. But you do not need to write algorithms like those to benefit from SIMD. The common case is dramatically simpler.

sentences.en: That's the basics.
sentences.zh: 这就是基础知识。

sentences.en: Projects such as simdutf and simdjson take this to an extreme and use SIMD techniques that can be difficult to understand.
sentences.zh: simdutf 和 simdjson 等项目将这一点发挥到了极致，并使用了难以理解的 SIMD 技术。

sentences.en: But you do not need to write algorithms like those to benefit from SIMD.
sentences.zh: 但您无需编写类似的算法即可从 SIMD 中受益。

sentences.en: The common case is dramatically simpler.
sentences.zh: 常见情况要简单得多。

blocks.html_en: The Common Shape

sentences.en: The Common Shape
sentences.zh: 常见形状

blocks.html_en: The common "process N values at a time" SIMD code follows the same five steps:

sentences.en: The common "process N values at a time" SIMD code follows the same five steps:
sentences.zh: 常见的“一次处理 N 个值”SIMD 代码遵循相同的五个步骤：

blocks.html_en: • Broadcast any constants you need and initialize vector accumulators, if any.

sentences.en: Broadcast any constants you need and initialize vector accumulators, if any.
sentences.zh: 广播您需要的任何常量并初始化向量累加器（如果有）。

blocks.html_en: • Loop over input one vector-width chunk at a time.

sentences.en: Loop over input one vector-width chunk at a time.
sentences.zh: 一次循环输入一个矢量宽度的块。

blocks.html_en: • Perform the comparison or arithmetic across all lanes in parallel.

sentences.en: Perform the comparison or arithmetic across all lanes in parallel.
sentences.zh: 在所有通道上并行执行比较或算术。

blocks.html_en: • Reduce or store the vector result as needed.

sentences.en: Reduce or store the vector result as needed.
sentences.zh: 根据需要减少或存储矢量结果。

blocks.html_en: • Handle the remaining elements with a scalar tail. A scalar tail is just your normal loop from before vectorizing, but it only processes the remainder that doesn't fit into a full vector.

sentences.en: Handle the remaining elements with a scalar tail.
sentences.zh: 使用标量尾部处理剩余元素。

sentences.en: A scalar tail is just your normal loop from before vectorizing, but it only processes the remainder that doesn't fit into a full vector.
sentences.zh: 标量尾部只是向量化之前的正常循环，但它只处理不适合完整向量的剩余部分。

blocks.html_en: As you do this more and more, you'll begin to naturally decompose every for loop into these five steps and writing SIMD becomes nearly as natural as writing a scalar loop.

sentences.en: As you do this more and more, you'll begin to naturally decompose every for loop into these five steps and writing SIMD becomes nearly as natural as writing a scalar loop.
sentences.zh: 随着您越来越多地这样做，您将开始自然地将每个 for 循环分解为这五个步骤，并且编写 SIMD 几乎与编写标量循环一样自然。

blocks.html_en: I have a slice of decoded codepoints that I want to consume until I see a value at or below 0xF (a C0 control character).2 Terminals are mostly plain characters to be printed, so we try to batch all those together. So this loop finds the end of the next printable run as quickly as possible.

sentences.en: So this loop finds the end of the next printable run as quickly as possible.

blocks.html_en: Here is the generic vector version with no CPU-specific intrinsics3 and no comments. I will explain it in detail later.

sentences.en: Here is the generic vector version with no CPU-specific intrinsics3 and no comments.
sentences.zh: 这是通用向量版本，没有特定于 CPU 的内在函数3，也没有注释。

blocks.html_en: This can improve the loop's throughput by up to 4x with ARM NEON (including Apple Silicon), 8x with AVX2 (most modern x86 CPUs), and 16x with AVX-512 (some Intel CPUs and AMD Zen 4 and newer).

sentences.en: This can improve the loop's throughput by up to 4x with ARM NEON (including Apple Silicon), 8x with AVX2 (most modern x86 CPUs), and 16x with AVX-512 (some Intel CPUs and AMD Zen 4 and newer).
sentences.zh: 这可以将 ARM NEON（包括 Apple Silicon）的循环吞吐量提高高达 4 倍，使用 AVX2（最现代的 x86 CPU）提高 8 倍，使用 AVX-512（某些 Intel CPU 和 AMD Zen 4 及更新版本）提高 16 倍。

blocks.html_en: In real-world end-to-end throughput from terminal program to finalized terminal state on an AVX2 Intel desktop, this was more like a 5x speedup. You always lose some of the ideal speedup due to the other stuff around the SIMD code, but... that's still 5x!

sentences.en: In real-world end-to-end throughput from terminal program to finalized terminal state on an AVX2 Intel desktop, this was more like a 5x speedup.
sentences.zh: 在 AVX2 Intel 桌面上从终端程序到最终终端状态的实际端到端吞吐量中，这更像是 5 倍的加速。

sentences.en: You always lose some of the ideal speedup due to the other stuff around the SIMD code, but...
sentences.zh: 由于 SIMD 代码周围的其他内容，您总是会失去一些理想的加速，但是......

sentences.en: that's still 5x!
sentences.zh: 这仍然是 5 倍！

blocks.html_en: simd.lanes(u32) is a helper in Ghostty that returns the number of u32 values the target CPU can process at once. These individual values are called lanes. On ARM this returns 4, AVX2 returns 8, and AVX-512 returns 16. If the target doesn't have a vector size we want to use, it returns null and we skip all of this code and do zero SIMD work.

sentences.en: simd.lanes(u32) is a helper in Ghostty that returns the number of u32 values the target CPU can process at once.
sentences.zh: simd.lanes(u32) 是 Ghostty 中的一个助手，它返回目标 CPU 一次可以处理的 u32 值的数量。

sentences.en: These individual values are called lanes.
sentences.zh: 这些单独的值称为车道。

sentences.en: On ARM this returns 4, AVX2 returns 8, and AVX-512 returns 16.
sentences.zh: 在 ARM 上返回 4，AVX2 返回 8，AVX-512 返回 16。

sentences.en: If the target doesn't have a vector size we want to use, it returns null and we skip all of this code and do zero SIMD work.
sentences.zh: 如果目标没有我们想要使用的向量大小，它将返回 null，我们会跳过所有这些代码并执行零 SIMD 工作。

blocks.html_en: @Vector(lanes, u32) creates the vector type. If lanes is 8, then V is a single value containing eight u32 values that the CPU can operate on in parallel. And so on.

sentences.en: @Vector(lanes, u32) creates the vector type.
sentences.zh: @Vector(lanes, u32) 创建向量类型。

sentences.en: If lanes is 8, then V is a single value containing eight u32 values that the CPU can operate on in parallel.
sentences.zh: 如果通道数为 8，则 V 是一个包含 CPU 可以并行操作的八个 u32 值的单个值。

sentences.en: And so on.
sentences.zh: 等等。

blocks.html_en: If lanes is 8, we only enter the loop when at least eight values remain. Inside the loop, we load those eight values into the vector values. At the end of every loop, end += lanes moves forward by eight values instead of one.

sentences.en: If lanes is 8, we only enter the loop when at least eight values remain.
sentences.zh: 如果车道数为 8，则只有在至少剩余 8 个值时才进入循环。

sentences.en: Inside the loop, we load those eight values into the vector values.
sentences.zh: 在循环内，我们将这八个值加载到向量值中。

sentences.en: At the end of every loop, end += lanes moves forward by eight values instead of one.
sentences.zh: 在每个循环结束时，end += 车道向前移动八个值而不是一个。

blocks.html_en: The requirement for a complete vector is important. If only five values remain, we can't load an eight-lane vector. There are various tricks to handle this, but we do the easy thing and handle them via our scalar tail, which I'll explain later in step 5.

sentences.en: The requirement for a complete vector is important.
sentences.zh: 对完整向量的要求很重要。

sentences.en: If only five values remain, we can't load an eight-lane vector.
sentences.zh: 如果只剩下五个值，我们就无法加载八通道向量。

sentences.en: There are various tricks to handle this, but we do the easy thing and handle them via our scalar tail, which I'll explain later in step 5.
sentences.zh: 有多种技巧可以处理这个问题，但我们做简单的事情并通过标量尾处理它们，我将在稍后的步骤 5 中解释。

The one > compares every lane in values to every corresponding lane in threshold. blocks.html_en: @reduce(.And, ...) combines every boolean using and and returns a single boolean. If every lane is true, we continue and process the next vector. In our example, lane 3 is false, so @reduce returns false and we fall through to find exactly which lane failed.

sentences.en: @reduce(.And, ...)
sentences.zh: @reduce(.并且，...)

sentences.en: combines every boolean using and and returns a single boolean.
sentences.zh: 使用 and 组合每个布尔值并返回一个布尔值。

sentences.en: If every lane is true, we continue and process the next vector.
sentences.zh: 如果每个通道都为真，我们将继续处理下一个向量。

sentences.en: In our example, lane 3 is false, so @reduce returns false and we fall through to find exactly which lane failed.
sentences.zh: 在我们的示例中，通道 3 为 false，因此 @reduce 返回 false，我们无法准确找到哪个通道失败。

blocks.html_en: @bitCast turns the vector of booleans into an integer with one bit per lane. A 1 bit means the value was greater than 0xF and a 0 means it wasn't. We invert the mask so failed comparisons are 1, and then @ctz counts the number of zero bits before the first failure. That count is the index of the first failing lane.

sentences.en: @bitCast turns the vector of booleans into an integer with one bit per lane.
sentences.zh: @bitCast 将布尔向量转换为每通道一位的整数。

sentences.en: A 1 bit means the value was greater than 0xF and a 0 means it wasn't.
sentences.zh: 1 位表示该值大于 0xF，0 表示不大于。

sentences.en: We invert the mask so failed comparisons are 1, and then @ctz counts the number of zero bits before the first failure.
sentences.zh: 我们反转掩码，因此失败的比较为 1，然后 @ctz 计算第一次失败之前的零位数。

sentences.en: That count is the index of the first failing lane.
sentences.zh: 该计数是第一个失败车道的索引。

blocks.html_en: We add that index to end and break because we found the control character.

sentences.en: We add that index to end and break because we found the control character.
sentences.zh: 我们将该索引添加到 end 并中断，因为我们找到了控制字符。

sentences.zh: 使用步骤 3 中的相同值，我们可以看到每个通道的转换：

blocks.html_en: @ctz(~mask) counts three zero bits before the first 1, so it returns 3. Adding 3 to end points it at lane 3, which contains 0x0A, the first control character.

sentences.en: @ctz(~mask) counts three zero bits before the first 1, so it returns 3.
sentences.zh: @ctz(~mask) 在第一个 1 之前计算三个 0 位，因此返回 3。

sentences.en: Adding 3 to end points it at lane 3, which contains 0x0A, the first control character.
sentences.zh: 向端点添加 3 将其指向通道 3，其中包含第一个控制字符 0x0A。

blocks.html_en: This is step 4: reduce the vector result into whatever the original algorithm needs. This is also the step that varies the most between algorithms. A sum might reduce a vector accumulator into a single number. A transform might store the entire vector to an output buffer. Our scan turns the vector into a bit mask so it can find one specific lane.

sentences.en: This is step 4: reduce the vector result into whatever the original algorithm needs.
sentences.zh: 这是第 4 步：将向量结果缩减为原始算法所需的值。

sentences.en: This is also the step that varies the most between algorithms.

content_html_zh:
SIMD 因复杂而闻名。 我遇到过许多非常优秀的软件工程师，他们认为它太复杂而难以学习，或者是一种只适用于最高性能软件的利基优化，在日常编程中没有用处。

我认为这是错误的。 SIMD 很容易理解1，并且用于加速简单 for 循环的常见“一次处理 N 个值”SIMD 代码几乎总是遵循相同的一般形状。 一旦学习了基础知识，编写 SIMD 就和编写 for 循环一样简单。 如果不是，通常是暂时跳过它的好兆头。

每个开发人员都应该至少了解这么多 SIMD。

这篇文章使用 Zig 作为示例，但它是适用于任何编程语言的通用文章。 对 SIMD 指令的支持因编程语言而异，我希望将来有更多的编程语言公开这些通用概念！

我讨厌现在每个帖子都必须这样做，但我也想指出这完全是手写的，没有人工智能的帮助。

Table of Contents
  • 背景：什么是 SIMD？
  • 常见形状
  • 一个真实的例子
  • 第 1 步：广播常量
  • 第 2 步：一次循环一个向量
  • 步骤 3：执行 SIMD 操作
  • 步骤 4：减少向量结果
  • 第 5 步：完成标量尾部
  • 回顾：常见形状
  • 为什么编译器不能这样做？
  • 每个人都应该了解 SIMD

背景：什么是 SIMD？

如果您已经知道 SIMD 是什么，请跳过本节。

SIMD 允许 CPU 并行操作多个值。 例如，CPU 可以使用一条指令比较 4、8 甚至更多字节，而不是一次比较一个字节。

如果您在代码中看到这样的循环：

for (byte in bytes) { /* ... */ }
for (character in string) { /* ... */ }
for (value in array) { /* ... */ }

有机会使用SIMD。 SIMD 将它们变成这样：

for (8 byte chunk in bytes) { /* ... */ }

这会导致局部加速，直接映射到并行性：您处理数据的速度提高了 4 倍、8 倍，甚至更快。

要获得回报，唯一真正的要求是您需要定期处理足够多的字节。 如果您在只有少数或几十个字节的数据上执行这些 for 循环，那么这是不值得的。 但如果迭代超过数百、数千、数百万字节，回报将是巨大的。

这就是基础知识。 simdutf 和 simdjson 等项目将这一点发挥到了极致，并使用了难以理解的 SIMD 技术。 但您无需编写类似的算法即可从 SIMD 中受益。 常见情况要简单得多。

常见形状

常见的“一次处理 N 个值”SIMD 代码遵循相同的五个步骤：

  1. 广播您需要的任何常量并初始化向量累加器（如果有）。
  2. 一次循环输入一个矢量宽度的块。
  3. 在所有通道上并行执行比较或算术。
  4. 根据需要减少或存储矢量结果。
  5. 使用标量尾部处理剩余元素。 标量尾部只是向量化之前的正常循环，但它只处理不适合完整向量的剩余部分。

随着您越来越多地这样做，您将开始自然地将每个 for 循环分解为这五个步骤，并且编写 SIMD 几乎与编写标量循环一样自然。

一个真实的例子

让我们看一个来自 Ghostty 的真实例子。 我们将研究标量实现、SIMD 实现，然后将其映射回上面的常见形状。

我有一段解码后的代码点，我想使用它，直到看到一个等于或低于 0xF（C0 控制字符）的值。2 终端大多是要打印的纯字符，因此我们尝试将所有这些字符批处理在一起。 所以这个循环会尽快找到下一个可打印运行的结尾。

标量循环是一行：

while (end < cps.len and cps[end] > 0xF) end += 1;

它一次处理一个代码点。 这很容易理解。

这是通用向量版本，没有特定于 CPU 的内在函数3，也没有注释。 稍后我会详细解释。

if (simd.lanes(u32)) |lanes| {
    const V = @Vector(lanes, u32);
    const threshold: V = @splat(0xF);
    while (end + lanes <= cps.len) : (end += lanes) {
        const values: V = cps[end..][0..lanes].*;
        const greater_than_threshold = values > threshold;
        if (@reduce(.And, greater_than_threshold)) continue;
        const mask: std.meta.Int(.unsigned, lanes) = @bitCast(greater_than_threshold);
        end += @ctz(~mask);
        break;
    }
}

while (end < cps.len and cps[end] > 0xF) end += 1;

又多了 12 行代码。

这可以将 ARM NEON（包括 Apple Silicon）的循环吞吐量提高高达 4 倍，使用 AVX2（最现代的 x86 CPU）提高 8 倍，使用 AVX-512（某些 Intel CPU 和 AMD Zen 4 及更新版本）提高 16 倍。

在 AVX2 Intel 桌面上从终端程序到最终终端状态的实际端到端吞吐量中，这更像是 5 倍的加速。 由于 SIMD 代码周围的其他内容，您总是会失去一些理想的加速，但是...... 这仍然是 5 倍！

好吧，现在我明白这 12 行对于不熟悉这些概念的人来说看起来非常陌生。 现在让我们逐步备份并解释它，将其直接映射到前面提到的形状。

第 1 步：广播常量

让我们从前三行开始：

if (simd.lanes(u32)) |lanes| {
    const V = @Vector(lanes, u32);
    const threshold: V = @splat(0xF);

simd.lanes(u32) 是 Ghostty 中的一个助手，它返回目标 CPU 一次可以处理的 u32 值的数量。 这些单独的值称为车道。 在 ARM 上返回 4，AVX2 返回 8，AVX-512 返回 16。 如果目标没有我们想要使用的向量大小，它将返回 null，我们会跳过所有这些代码并执行零 SIMD 工作。

@Vector(lanes, u32) 创建向量类型。 如果通道数为 8，则 V 是一个包含 CPU 可以并行操作的八个 u32 值的单个值。 等等。

最后，我们需要将每个值与 0xF 进行比较。 矢量比较需要两侧都有一个矢量，因此 @splat(0xF) 将 0xF 复制或广播到每个通道中。 结果是一个如下所示的向量：

{ 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF, 0xF }

这是第 1 步：准备向量类型并广播所有常量。 有些算法也会在这里初始化一个向量累加器，但这个算法不需要。

第 2 步：一次循环一个向量

接下来，我们一次循环一个完整的向量：

while (end + lanes <= cps.len) : (end += lanes) {
    const values: V = cps[end..][0..lanes].*;

如果车道数为 8，则只有在至少剩余 8 个值时才进入循环。 在循环内，我们将这八个值加载到向量值中。 在每个循环结束时，end += 车道向前移动八个值而不是一个。

对完整向量的要求很重要。 如果只剩下五个值，我们就无法加载八通道向量。 有多种技巧可以处理这个问题，但我们做简单的事情并通过标量尾处理它们，我将在稍后的步骤 5 中解释。

这是第 2 步：一次加载并循环输入一个矢量宽度的块。 您可以在这里看到车道数加速！

步骤 3：执行 SIMD 操作

现在我们进行比较：

const greater_than_threshold = values > threshold;

值和阈值都是向量，因此这映射到向量运算（文字向量 CPU 指令）。 > 将每个通道的值与每个相应通道的阈值进行比较。 如果有八个通道，这相当于执行标量比较 cps[end] > 0xF 八次，但它是在一条 CPU 指令中完成的。4

结果是另一个向量，每条通道有一个布尔值。 从概念上讲，它看起来像这样：

values:                 { 0x41, 0x42, 0x43, 0x0A, 0x44, 0x45, 0x46, 0x47 }
threshold:              {  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF,  0xF }
greater_than_threshold: { true, true, true, false, true, true, true, true }

这是实际的 SIMD 操作。 没有明确的内循环。 > 运算符并行应用于每个通道。

比较只是一个例子。 这可以是加法、乘法、最小值、最大值或向量类型支持的任何其他运算。 关键是代码仍然具有相同的形状。

步骤 4：减少向量结果

我们现在有一个布尔向量，但原始循环需要知道第一个值等于或低于 0xF 的位置。

首先，让我们处理每个值都高于 0xF 的常见情况：

if (@reduce(.And, greater_than_threshold)) continue;

@reduce(.并且，...) 使用 and 组合每个布尔值并返回一个布尔值。 如果每个通道都为真，我们将继续处理下一个向量。 在我们的示例中，通道 3 为 false，因此 @reduce 返回 false，我们无法准确找到哪个通道失败。

如果任何通道错误，那么我们需要准确找到哪条通道失败：

const mask: std.meta.Int(.unsigned, lanes) = @bitCast(greater_than_threshold);
end += @ctz(~mask);
break;

@bitCast 将布尔向量转换为每通道一位的整数。 1 位表示该值大于 0xF，0 表示不大于。 我们反转掩码，因此失败的比较为 1，然后 @ctz 计算第一次失败之前的零位数。 该计数是第一个失败车道的索引。

我们将该索引添加到 end 并中断，因为我们找到了控制字符。

使用步骤 3 中的相同值，我们可以看到每个通道的转换：

values:                 { 0x41, 0x42, 0x43, 0x0A, 0x44, 0x45, 0x46, 0x47 }
greater_than_threshold: { true, true, true, false, true, true, true, true }
mask:                   {    1,    1,    1,     0,    1,    1,    1,    1 }
~mask:                  {    0,    0,    0,     1,    0,    0,    0,    0 }

@ctz(~mask) 在第一个 1 之前计算三个 0 位，因此返回 3。 向端点添加 3 将其指向通道 3，其中包含第一个控制字符 0x0A。

这是第 4 步：将向量结果缩减为原始算法所需的值。 这也是算法之间差异最大的步骤。 求和可以将向量累加器减少为单个数字。 转换可能会将整个向量存储到输出缓冲区。 我们的扫描将向量转换为位掩码，以便它可以找到一个特定的车道。

第 5 步：完成标量尾部

在矢量循环之后，我们运行与开始时完全相同的标量循环：

while (end < cps.len and cps[end] > 0xF) end += 1;

如果输入长度不是向量宽度的精确倍数，则会处理剩余的值。 例如，八通道向量循环为此循环留下零到七个值。 这称为标量尾部。

此循环还处理 simd.lanes(u32) 返回 null 的 CPU。 在这种情况下，我们会跳过所有 SIMD 代码，并且标量循环会处理整个输入。 最初的实现仍然是后备和尾部。

这是第 5 步。 这只是正常的循环。

回顾：常见形状

让我们将整个实现映射回五个步骤：

  1. @splat(0xF) 将比较值广播到每个通道。
  2. while 循环一次加载通道值。
  3. 值 > 阈值并行比较每个通道。
  4. @reduce、@bitCast 和 @ctz 查找第一个失败的比较。
  5. 原始标量循环处理剩余的和不支持的 CPU。

第 4 步中的细节最初需要一些时间来理解，但总体形状很简单。 在完全不同的算法中，步骤 1、2、3 和 5 看起来几乎相同。

每当您看到 for（以字节为单位）时，这就是您将映射到的形状。

为什么编译器不能这样做？

有时可以！ 编译器可以自动向量化简单循环，特别是没有复杂控制流的常规算术循环。 您应该始终通过优化来编译标量版本，并在手动编写 SIMD 之前查看编译器生成的内容。

但编译器在自动向量化方面受到严重限制，而且通常在这方面表现很差。 几十年来，自动矢量化一直是编译器研究的一个活跃领域，最近的研究仍然始于生产编译器经常错过矢量化机会的观察。 我预计这个问题不会很快消失。

更重要的是，当这个循环对我来说足够重要以至于关心 5 倍加速时，我希望矢量化是明确且可预测的。 我不希望不相关的代码更改或编译器更新悄悄地将其转回标量循环。

每个人都应该了解 SIMD

每个开发人员都应该能够认识到这个机会，最重要的是，不应该害怕 SIMD。 如果您看到热循环扫描、比较、计数或转换大量连续数据，您应该能够想象一次处理一个向量宽度的块。

这篇文章表明，这些常见情况遵循一种非常规律的模式，您很快就会习惯。 凭借良好的语言支持，您无需了解任何汇编或特定于 CPU 的怪癖即可轻松改进。

每个人都应该足够了解 SIMD 来做到这一点。5

  2. C0 控件超出 0xF。 这是 Ghostty 用于此特定代码路径的截止值； ESC 和其他控制序列处理发生在其他地方。 ↩
  3. 通用向量删除了特定于 CPU 的语法，而不是特定于 CPU 的代码生成。 Zig 仍然将这些操作降低到为目标启用的指令集。 当 Ghostty 无法选择支持的向量宽度时，它会退回到标量代码。 ↩
  4. 比较本身就是一种向量运算。 加载向量、减少结果以及定位故障通道需要额外的指令。 重要的是我们同时进行多次比较。 ↩
